findings.subject: active-owner.json deletion
findings.monitor_question: Was the active-owner deletion intentional housekeeping or a claim bug?

evidence.cleanup_criteria: dead PID + older than 4 hours = stale

findings.explanation: The active-owner.json claimed by session sess_mp22b0v8_418804 (PID 418804) had been dead for ~6.9 hours and was 6.75 hours past its 15-minute expiry. The store-journal.js autofix --apply command identified this as stale using its published criteria (dead PID + age > 4h) and deleted the file. This is standard housekeeping, not a claim bug. No current session was displaced — the current heartbeat session (sess-1778580541786-b875da94, origin opencode) never claimed active-owner because no exclusive work was being performed.

findings.subject: journal gap of 3 days

evidence.root_cause: May 11-12 session did significant work but never wrote journal entries before committing. May 10 was a true idle day.

remediation.2026-05-10.jsonl: Created with idle-day marker entries (no Library work that day)
remediation.2026-05-11.jsonl: Created with backfilled work_started and work_completed entries documenting schema fix, script bugfixes, packet review, NACK cleanup, and commit d521072

findings.explanation: The 3-day journal gap was caused by a session that performed work but failed to write journal entries before its git commit. This has been remediated by creating per-day journal files for May 10 (idle) and May 11 (backfilled from session records and git history). The journal is now continuous from May 7 onward.

actions_taken: Ran store-journal.js autofix --apply to clean stale active-owners
Rewrote 2026-05-12.jsonl with only current session entries (removed misfiled backfills)
next_commit: Will include this evidence file + journal reorganization
